Name: semantic_rate_distortion_compression
Title: Semantic Rate-Distortion Compression: Bridging Information Theory and Model Interpretability
Short Hypothesis: Incorporating semantic awareness into rate-distortion frameworks can significantly improve model compression efficiency while preserving task-relevant information, yielding lower distortion in practical applications.
Related Work: While existing works have explored rate-distortion approaches for model compression (Gao et al., 2018; Young, 2025), they often neglect the semantic integrity of the compressed model. The proposed research integrates semantic constraints into the rate-distortion framework, distinguishing itself by focusing on the preservation of task-relevant features while achieving compression.
Abstract: Model compression, essential for deploying deep learning models in resource-constrained environments, has primarily focused on reducing bit rates and distortions without considering the semantic relevance of the compressed representations. This proposal introduces a novel approach to model compression that integrates semantic awareness into the rate-distortion framework. By defining a new distortion measure that accounts for semantic fidelity in addition to traditional signal distortion, we seek to optimize the trade-off between compression rate and task performance. The proposed method will involve formulating a new objective that combines mutual information metrics with semantic loss functions, allowing for the systematic allocation of bits to preserve essential task-relevant features. We will validate our approach through experiments on benchmark datasets, demonstrating improved performance in semantic tasks compared to traditional compression methods. This research will bridge the gap between theoretical information measures and practical model performance.
Experiments: Define a novel semantic distortion metric based on mutual information and semantic similarity measures.
Apply this metric in a rate-distortion optimization framework to compress standard benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet).
Compare the performance of the proposed method against existing rate-distortion-based compression methods using accuracy, F1-score, and semantic similarity metrics as evaluation criteria.
Conduct ablation studies to analyze the impact of varying semantic fidelity in compression.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed semantic distortion measure may introduce additional complexity in the optimization process.
The extent of benefit from semantic awareness may vary across different tasks and datasets.
Initial experiments may not yield significant improvements over existing methods, requiring iterative refinement of the approach.
